selected_important_turn.text: Well, I'll start with a trial offer and we'll go from there
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn signals willingness to bargain and frames the interaction as iterative/cooperative — it sets the negotiation's tone and gives permission to propose concrete trades.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It's only an announcement of intent (a meta-offer) rather than a concrete proposal or a targeted question. That leaves the initiative vague and misses an early chance to shape a mutually beneficial package or to elicit the opponent's precise priorities/constraints.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Instead of only announcing a trial offer, move immediately to a specific, testable proposal or a focused question (e.g., propose a concrete split or ask which item they value most/how much water they need). This channels the cooperative intent into actionable terms.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (more chance to identify integrative trades earlier)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (more concrete terms reduce ambiguity about division)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (reduces back-and-forth by offering a starting point)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: small increase (demonstrates constructive leadership while remaining cooperative)

value2reason.Low: Water is important because you can die without water quicker than food so I don't know why this isn't a high priority item
value2reason.Medium: Firewood is important because it cooks food, feeps you warm, provide light, and scares animals away
value2reason.High: Food is very important to stay alive, and keep up the energy

value2reason.Low: It gets very cold at nght
value2reason.Medium: Little food or vegetation for food
value2reason.High: Camping in a desert with no water supplies

dialogue.text: I'm good! I'm looking forward to my camping trip this weekend. How about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm good as well, I hope the weather works with me 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Sending good luck your way! Any items in particular you need for your trip?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Well, I'll start with a trial offer and we'll go from there
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think we can split the supplies fairly. How about I take 2 Food, 1 Firewood, and 1 Water, and you get the rest? It balances our priorities and ensures we both have enough to stay safe.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That split doesn't align with my needs. I need more water since we're in a desert. How about I take 2 Water, 1 Food, and 1 Firewood, and you get the rest? It's fair and meets my priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see you need more water. Let's adjust. How about I give you 2 Water, 1 Food, and 1 Firewood, while I take 1 Food, 2 Firewood, and 1 Water? It balances our needs and ensures we both have enough to stay safe.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need more water than that. How about I take 3 Water, 1 Food, and 1 Firewood, while you take 2 Food and 2 Firewood? It better reflects my priority for water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand the need for more water. How about I give you 3 Water, 1 Food, and 1 Firewood, while I take 2 Food and 2 Firewood? This way, we both meet our priorities, and all supplies are allocated.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That works for me. Final split: I take 3 Water, 1 Food, 1 Firewood. You get 2 Food, 2 Firewood. All packages are allocated. Let’s head out.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
